description: Curated drop-in training scenarios (data-driven; merged at load time).

records.title: Grease fire at home
records.briefing: You are cooking when a pan of oil ignites on the stovetop. Flames rise toward the cabinets. Act calmly and correctly under pressure.

cues.text: Oil in the pan is on fire
Cabinets above the stove are close to the flames
There is a sink full of water nearby (tempting but dangerous)
A metal lid sits on the counter

records.decision_prompt: What is your first action sequence? State priorities, not every step.
emergency_steps: Turn off the heat if you can reach it safely
Smother the flames with a metal lid or baking soda - never water
If it spreads, get out and call emergency services
Do not move the burning pan
foresight_prompts: What happens if you throw water on burning oil?
What is your exit if it spreads?
critical_thinking_prompts: Why is water dangerous on a grease fire?
When do you stop fighting and evacuate?

records.title: Bystander intervention - public harassment
records.briefing: On a crowded train platform, you witness a person being verbally harassed. Others look away. You can help safely.

cues.text: The aggressor is loud but not physically violent yet
Several bystanders are present but passive
A staffed help point is 20 meters away

records.decision_prompt: How do you intervene safely in the next 60 seconds?
emergency_steps: Assess safety - do not escalate to violence
Use the 5 Ds: Distract, Delegate, Document, Delay, Direct
Support the targeted person; ask what they need
Get help from staff or call authorities if it escalates
foresight_prompts: What if the aggressor turns on you?
How do you keep the target safe if it escalates?
critical_thinking_prompts: Which of the 5 Ds is safest here?

records.title: Winter power outage at home
records.briefing: A severe winter storm knocks out power overnight. Temperatures are dropping fast and you are responsible for a household including an older relative.

cues.text: A portable generator is in the garage
An older relative is sensitive to cold
The refrigerator is full of perishable food

records.decision_prompt: What are your priorities for the first hour?
emergency_steps: Never run a generator indoors or in the garage - carbon monoxide risk
Conserve heat: close off rooms, layer clothing, use blankets
Keep refrigerator/freezer closed to preserve food
Check on vulnerable household members frequently
foresight_prompts: What is the danger of running the generator inside?
How long will food stay safe?
critical_thinking_prompts: What is the deadliest mistake here?
How do you balance warmth and ventilation?
